stem: A release enters the software release pipeline. The probability of passing code review is 91%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing unit testing is between 76% and 91% (uncertain due to evaluator variability). If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing integration testing is 96%. If the previous stage is passed, the probability of passing deployment is 85%. What is the overall probability that the release is deployed without incidents?

ground_truth.answer: P(deployed without incidents) ranges from 0.564 to 0.676 as the unit testing pass rate varies from 76% to 91%. Point estimate: 0.616.

solution_steps: The release must pass all 4 stages sequentially: P(success) = P(stage 1)×P(stage 2)×P(stage 3)×P(stage 4).
The uncertainty in the unit testing stage creates a 11.2 percentage point range in the overall success probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential stages
Using a single value for the unit testing rate without acknowledging uncertainty
Not recognizing that uncertainty at one stage propagates through all subsequent stages
